chapter_title.ar: باب مِيرَاثِ ذَوِي الْأَرْحَامِ مَعَ الْمَوَالِي
chapter_title.en: Chapter 32 – Inheritance of the relatives with the Mawali (slave)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/200)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/200)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَيُّ شَيْ‏ءٍ لِلْمَوَالِي مِنَ الْمِيرَاثِ فَقَالَ لَيْسَ لَهُمْ شَيْ‏ءٌ إِلَّا التَّرْبَاءُ يَعْنِي التُّرَابَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazal, from Ibn Abu Al Hamra’a who said,
‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Which thing from the inheritance, is for the Mawla (Master)?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is nothing for them except for the soil, meaning dust’.
en.sarwar: 4. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from ibn abu al-Hamra’ who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about what Mawla’ (Master) receives of inheritance. He (the Imam) said, ‘They receive nothing except soil, that is, dirt.’”
